messages.content: today was just a lot. it's the anniversary of my mom passing and i didn't really tell anyone i was struggling with it

That's a heavy day to carry quietly. Grief on an anniversary can hit differently — sharper, sometimes, than you expect.

What's it been like, holding that to yourself today?
thanks for just sitting with me on it. i don't usually let myself actually cry but i think i needed to

It makes sense that it came through today. Grief finds its way out eventually — and letting it is not weakness, even when it feels unfamiliar.

Is there anything about her you've been wanting to say out loud, that's been sitting with you?
i'm still pretty heavy ngl, it hasn't really lifted. but i've got an early start and i don't wanna keep going tonight, i think i just need to stop

That sounds like the right call — you've carried a lot today, and rest is part of it too.

Take care of yourself tonight.
yeah. gonna go lie down in the dark for a while. night
Good night. She was worth grieving.
